title: The Werewolf & the Village
subtitle: Svalich Road · The Village of Barovia · The Tavern
opener: The road between the Death House and the village did not offer a quiet walk. A pack of wolves emerged from the treeline, and with them something larger — a werewolf. Not feral, not random. It moved with the deliberate coordination of something receiving orders, and when the group finally brought it down, that impression did not go away. Strahd was still testing them. This was not the last test.

paragraphs: They arrived at the Village of Barovia at dusk. The mist sat low between the houses. Windows were shuttered. Doors were bolted. The few silhouettes they glimpsed disappeared the moment they sensed movement on the road. This is a village that learned long ago that strangers after dark are not a good sign.

sections.heading: The Tavern & Ismark
paragraphs: The only light came from the tavern. Inside they found Ismark Kolyanovich — son of the dead burgomaster, broad-shouldered, carrying the weight of a village on his face. He recognised immediately that they were not from Barovia, and he did not waste time. His father was dead. His sister Ireena was in danger. The attacks on the mansion had been escalating. He needed help he could not find here.

Kaelos, true to form, found his own point of interest in the tavern — a Vistani woman named Mirabel who decided he was worth her attention. She made no effort to be subtle about it, and when she extended an invitation to the Vistani camp by the river, it was impossible to tell whether it was personal interest or something else entirely. Kaelos filed this away with a smile.

sections.heading: The Burgomaster's Mansion
paragraphs: Ismark walked them to the mansion. In the dark, the damage was visible enough: deep claw marks in the doors, gouges in the shutters, tracks in the mud of the garden that didn't belong to anything natural. Whatever had been coming here was not subtle. It was not trying to be. The attacks were a message as much as an assault, and the message was directed at whoever was inside.
The session ended here, at the threshold of the mansion, with the evidence of Strahd's attention written into every scarred surface.

known: The werewolf on the road was under Strahd's control
Ireena Kolyana is being actively targeted by Strahd's forces
Ismark wants the group to escort Ireena to Vallaki
There is a Vistani camp by the river, west of the village — Mirabel extended an invitation to Kaelos
unanswered: Why is Strahd so fixated on Ireena specifically?
Why did Mirabel invite Kaelos to the camp — personal interest or something more deliberate?
people: Mirabel — Vistani woman at the tavern, invited Kaelos to the camp by the river
